title: quisqualis parvifolia
intro: Quisqualis parvifolia, also known as the Indian plum or Rabbula, is a tropical flowering plant native to the Western Ghats mountain range in India. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant has several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating cough and respiratory issues, and treating digestive problems. Quisqualis parvifolia can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and poultices to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
